extraction.one_line_summary: VINGEGAARD attacked early on the 4.8km final climb at 9% gradient, dropped KUSS by 29 seconds and likely ROGLIC, moving into second overall.
tactical_patterns: VINGEGAARD attacked on steepest gradients in second half of 4.8km climb, abandoning typical Jumbo-Visma controlled tempo strategy.
GROVES drove breakaway tempo relentlessly pre-intermediate sprint despite being pure sprinter, exploiting team weakness before auto-boost abandonment.
JUMBO-VISMA sent TRATNIK solo across to breakaway as psychological tactic, demoralizing break by using 'weakest guy' to bridge.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: attacked aggressively early on final climb contrary to typical support role, strongest rider entering third week
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: negative: lost 29 seconds to teammate on 4.8km climb after recuperating mid-effort, vulnerability exposed for Angliru stage
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: expected sprint finish setup negated by VINGEGAARD aggression, dropped to third overall behind teammate
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: sat up and lost significant time, avoiding Jumbo-Visma chase attention suggests prioritizing stage hunting over GC
